park_name: Brownhill Creek Tourist Park - Mitcham, South Australia

reviews.review_text: The Wi-Fi here is a complete joke.
Great little hideaway in the Adelaide Hills. Telephone and internet signal a bit sketchy but would stay again. Great walking trails.

Very old, very tired, the new a/C unit into our room was not fitted properly, night one wasp got in, we assumed we left door open, 2nd night another wasp, complaint fell on deaf ears. I am allergic to wasps. We will never shadow their door step again

The lack of WiFi ruins the experience. I saw earlier that management says is a challenge due to location. I disagree. It is entirely possible to install better WiFi mesh outdoor system to cover the cabins better.

I’m with Telstra and have a poor connection
Set up in a hidden valley. A beautiful place with lots of lovely Bird song. Phone Reception was limited to the front gate area was a bit of a task.

I don't recommend this park as you may or may not get what you've booked. If you look at the photos of powered sites on the website you'll see large, level sites with gravel and lawn or fake grass, which are very deceptive: I've included a photo of the type of site they advertise, and then the second photo is the site I was given. It was the same price ($60!) but I'd call it an "overflow" site. It was away from all the standard sites and the amenities block (which was clean), down the end of the park and sloping down to the creek behind. It was cold, dark and dank compared to the standard sites, and with many more mozzies. I had to stay inside the van but there was no reception for my phone, my tv antenna or my Starlink (SOS only on my phone). They should have some kind of booster down there as no reception at all in a capital city is not acceptable in 2026.

Lovely, quiet location. Lots of trees, attracting thousands of birds. The birdsong in the morning is the best alarm clock!
Friendly service and great facilities. There is even a cafe on site which sells excellent coffee, breakfast, brunch and lunch. Good, solid accommodation at reasonable prices. I'll definitely go back and spend longer next time.

Stayed in one of the studio units.
The unit was clean and deco lovely.
The park is great and dog friendly.
The staff was friendly and helpful.
No service to the unit for it was a travel auction and didn’t need servicing for 3 days.
Lovely walking trail close by.
The little cafe onsite was quaint and served nice food and coffee.
We were happy to hear the birds, see the koalas and a big kangaroo grazing on the hill.
Wifi was poor but not a worry for us.
Hotel close by for a nice meal.

Location is beautiful. However lack of wifi is really unacceptable these days. Cabins are clean and basic and could do with a revamp but that also semi reflects the price.
The unacceptable issue we had however was arriving to check in at 2.55pm, bearing in mind, check in is only between 2pm and 5pm, to find the office closed and a note on the door saying back at 3.40pm. There is an after hours lockbox, this was not used. We sat and waited for 35 minutes. Luckily for us they returned 10 minutes earlier than expected. Honestly, due to this, I would never recommend this park or stay there again.
The owners replied to my review above by saying they were attending to an issue within the park. This is simply not true. The lady working in the office had gone to pick up her son from school. They also said they encourage patrons to call in this case. I called approx 6 times. There was an automated answer that eventually rang out. I spoke to a gentleman I saw coming out of the staff area at about 3.20pm, he told me “she had gone to collect her son from school” he also made it sound like a regular occurrence. He said he would get his keys and see what he could do for us and then he disappeared. We did not see him again. The owners reply to my review is extremely disappointing as it is a fabrication and misleading. I have edited my review to add this and to remove my 1 star for service.
Beautiful surroundings. Nice and quiet. Service polite and helpful.

The only problem is the internet.
Very bad black hole.
If you need to work from here i advise against it.
Internet not stable or reliable
So quiet and we loved our studio.
Great spot,first time there.Plenty of bird life.

Always a good experience.
Close to Adelaide.

Great spot to stay, very clean facilities, great management team.
Only negative is the very poor mobile phone cover.

Excellent site very close to Adelaide for convenient city access. Amenities are fantastic - clean bathrooms with large shower cubicles and pool. Campsite is alongside the road, and some local kids were coming in doing skids on their bikes right beside campers, stirring up dust. Bring a drill for pegs, the ground is hard.

Honestly, I was a little disappointed with the staff. They seemed friendly. But then one of the staff members, Bel, was extremely nosy. She seemed concerned that I had been staying there a "long time", when it hadn't even been 2 weeks. No one else was treated that way. Even though a guy had been living there for 12 years! Haha!

It was only because I had a small tent, whereas other patrons generally had bigger setups. It was like she was concerned for my safety unnecessarily, and judgemental because I was an indenpendent traveller, and female.

Also, when my food was chucked out, even though it was clearly labelled, Bel gave me this thousand-yard stare, and didn't even apologise on behalf of the staff member responsible. She said absolutely nothing about it. Pathetic service.

Another female staff member was also very rude, when I asked to stay in the park a little longer to charge my phone. It was check out day, and she was very strict with the timing, and treated me like riff raff, just for politely asking.

Every single hostel I've been to, has been flexible with timing.

Also, two older male grounds workers, had this extremely creepy look whenever they saw me. I felt uncomfortable using the communal kitchen at some points. And a third older gentlemen, was keeping an eye on me. Without my knowledge. He kept saying that he noticed which tent I was from, repeatedly.

I am considered an attractive person, and I just didn't feel safe sometimes. I wish they hired better staff, instead of low-lives, with nothing better to do than being nosy.

It's a nice area, with plenty of butterflies 🙂 but I was extremely disappointed with the lack of emotional intelligence, and accountability in this place.
Lovely break from the Summer Heat... Staff Watering Gardens... Recycling Available... AoK
Fantastic little park, loads of shade on a hot day, the ensuite site had the best shower ever! Beautiful green lawn thanks to the lovely caretakers, will definitely be back!

We booked an unpowered site at the back of the park. Great location in Mitcham. But had a few gripes about the facilities and maintenance in unpowered section.  Very 3rd class area of the park. No gravel on path/road in so very dusty. Especially when it got windy. Toilet facilities very basic and floor never looked clean and needed a mop for showers as always wet floor.
Camp kitchen was pathetic .. not even a power point but the one down in powered area very well catered but 500m walk to get there.
I'd only go back if powered site.

We stayed in our caravan, I want to say what a great Caravan Park & the service we got at the office was fantastic. I am not sure of the persons name I think her name was Bell she went all out to help us with everything during our stay
I would fully recommend the Caravan Park to stay and know that it is a bery friendly Park
Beautiful.place qujet relaxing and plenty of single women great time there ♡♡♡♡☆☆☆☆☆
Great place peaceful native animals people are helpful

Great little caravan park not too far out of the city, but feels like your in the bush! Cabins had all we needed for a comfortable caravan park stay. Hiking trails and a dog park nearby. Cafe on-site, pool, playground, camp kitchen and laundry make this park a functional and fun place to stay at. We'll definitely be back. Dog friendly- on leash.
Very happy with our accommodation.
An awesome park only 10klms from city centre but in a beautiful nature reserve.Modern facilities are well maintained
Nice green area, friendly staff, cell phone reception is poor though.
It is  the best of both worlds close to nature with bundles of history near by.

We recently stayed here for a four day holiday and whilst the staff were lovely, the cabin was definitely not value for money. It’s extremely disappointing how there are so many reviews about the cleanliness being poor and the owners “being told” and yet nothing is changing. We booked into a two bedroom cabin and I am so glad we had our own bedding and cutlery because I would not have wanted to use any of what was provided.

Cleanliness of the cabin was extremely poor. There was mould on the roof of the shower, the walls and doors were peeling, dust and cobwebs on the curtains, food residue within the couch, the floor was sticky and filthy, cutlery was not clean at all and the smart tv couldn’t even access the internet onsite so the only available tv was standard channels. We had the groundsman come and look at the tv only for him to be told by reception that it doesn’t work with the internet. If you want to sit and relax and watch subscription tv, forget it. Unless you bring your own wifi, the internet onsite doesn’t allow for much and phone reception is extremely poor.

On the plus side there is a lovely cafe onsite that serves great coffee and banana bread and the staff are fantastic.

This is my second time staying and my families first and unfortunately will probably be our last  based on our recent stay. It is a real shame because the location is incredible.
Good place for holiday clean and quiet.
Great helpful management, ok for big motor homes and caravan with care, good quiet rural atmosphere by the creek, yet handy to the city.

Stayed on a unpowered site and there was a hobo that came and slept in a tent behind us. Whaling and screaming at the top of their lungs. We were so freaked out we left after two hours of being there. Calling brown hill creek to ask for a refund as we didn’t use any of the ammenities and were scared for our lives. They said no one else complained, can’t do anything and hung up. Just went back to check in the afternoon and the tent is still there.

We stayed in the caravan park for 10days in one of the cabin. It was nice but it's not as clean as we expected. There's some candy wrappers in one of the room and alot of dust in the areas which it looks like the management didn't clean it properly. The cabin itself is good but I think it needs more love and care.
Tracy & Bushy couldn't be more welcoming, friendly and basically amazing. Fabulous park, great location, brilliantly managed. See you next time around! 🙏

Look it could have been better, The cabins aren’t clean,  you can wipe the floor and it will still come back up black and are dirty and in poor condition , dirty curtains, filthy windowsills and are very outdated! There is NO phone reception, the wifi does not work at all. The staff ( I think Tracey)  are rude and not helpful and if there’s an issues or wanting a refund, you won’t get one. Only good thing is that it’s in a nice location and not far from the city. There’s also a lot of safety issues that are out of date or need to be fixed too. Wouldn’t recommend unless it’s the very last option you have!

I’d hoped the recent reviews of grubby sheets and towels might mean more effort was taken to improve things, but unfortunately we encountered the same. And the cabin was generally not very clean and in need of upgrades. Would have expected more for the price point.

Lovely spot though, and we found it great that the playground and cafe were so close to our cabin. Although the cafe opening times didn’t match with what is listed, and the food was disappointing, it was nice to be able to get a coffee.

Be aware that even with Telstra, we had almost no phone signal and the wifi is poor.
Loved our stay here. Excellent facilities, super helpful staff and so easy to get to and from the city via public transport. Highly, highly recommend.
Love this park. It is a little slice of paradise in the city.
nice park on the edge of the City

Great spot.... i was a little optimistic at first but the sites were large, had drive through and every site had artificial  grass.  Unpowered sites were great and plenty of walking tracks near by.  Also had a great coffee shop on site.  Everything  was clean, neat and tidy.

Lovely little caravan park. They have recently upgraded the camp kitchen and pool area with wooden decks etc. The cafe is very nice. Very nice little creek to walk around. There are new cabins ours was one of the older one which was dated but still nice.
Great park inside national park. Walking tracks start right there. Great clean facilities.
Lady at the reception Ally couldn't have been more helpful with everything and beautiful surroundings and great bird life highly recommend staying there 😀

If you're looking for a truly Aussie experience, look no further than Brownhill Creek Caravan Park. I’d give it five stars if for nothing else than the doonas – yes, they’re Australian flags! You can't get more patriotic than curling up under the Southern Cross after a day of exploring.

Beyond the doona novelty, the park itself is clean, peaceful, and beautifully nestled in nature. You get the convenience of being close to Adelaide with the serenity of a bush setting – the best of both worlds. Whether you're camping, caravanning, or staying in a cabin, it's a relaxing and charming spot to unwind.

Friendly staff, well-maintained facilities, and a unique touch of national pride in the bedding? That’s a win all around. Highly recommended for travelers wanting comfort with a true Aussie twist.

Occupied a powered caravan site. The park is on the fringe of Adelaide,  so not far to a shopping centre, yet we are in a bush like setting.  Nice walks and coffee shop on site. Getting expensive now coming into warmer weather.

Oh my what can I say about this place.
Everything was just great. Sites big. Trees bigger. Birds gorgeous. Yes amenities were good. Laundry lovely. Just stay there you'll love it.

Great quiet location out of the way of traffic noise and roads. Park like surrounds and a lovely mountain valley. So happy to finally find a place to get a good nice sleep in Adelaide. Cabins do need upgrading. There is investment in other areas of the parks infrastructure. Housekeeping does need to pick its act up though. Lots of cleaning failures in the cabin I rented. Good choices of accommodation types to choose from.

Lovely park with great staff.  Flat van sites and easy access to bathroom facilities.  It's only 7 minutes to Adelaide CBD so it makes a great base for traveling around the area.  We had lunch at the small cafe on the premises but its a bit expensive for what we got.
Such a beautiful spot. Loved the walks.
A good park. Good staff and spotless facilities. In a beautiful location.

Cabin 15 is a 3 bedroom cabin....but you cannot fit a port a cot in any of the bedrooms.
WiFi and phone signal bad due to being in a valley.

Other than that...will be definitely staying again.
Great for an overnight or longer. Very quiet. Most efficient and helpful staff. Reasonable rates.
Feels like country, but so close to city. Limited children entertainment but great spot for adult camper's with on-site cafe.
Best place to park your van in Adelaide. Great facilities and lovely bush location and very close to centre of Adelaide
Great place to stay would recommend this place to everyone

We stayed in a powered site, lovely artificial grassed area to pull our van up to. Incredibly beautiful time of year to visit as all the autumn leaves filled the park. As others have mentioned although you are 15 minutes from the CBD you wouldn't know it. Staff were friendly, attentive and facilities were clean. Nice little playground for our daughter, trendy little cafe on site. Family orientated and pet friendly. We will definitely stay here again.
Beautiful park ,plenty of walking tracks friendly people. Dogs aloud in cabins.

We had a dog friendly cabin.  Pretty basic and tatty, felt a bit grubby, definitely needs an upgrade for the price. Beds and pillows were very uncomfortable. On saying that the receptionist very friendly and helpful and the grounds beautiful, lovely spot to stay with a caravan.

Budget cabin was in very poor condition ie dirty sheets, towels   quilt covers where the bunk beds are..
Had to clean cupboard in the kitchen, dirty cup, knives and forks were greasy, I have sent them an Email but no reply yet, wasn't worth $240 for the 2 nights..Caravan park is lovely though! But unfortunately my Daughter Karli and I won't be back
Love staying with our caravan here.  This time we stayed overnight in a unit.  It was lovely and comfortable.  Would recommend to anyone needing a peaceful setting.
Great, quiet caravan park.

Had a very disappointing experience with this caravan park. On our last day/night we had left the park for an outing and received a call that our dog was left off a lead. I asked as we were out if someone could put our dog on a lead for us as it was inconvenient for us to come back, however if they couldn’t for whatever reason we would return to do so. Twenty minutes later we received a call saying they couldn’t find a lead (even though it was there) and also advised us we had been reported for animal cruelty for leaving our dogs without water. We returned to the park to put our dog on a lead. Luckily our dog is well trained and stayed at our campsite, however I fear what may have happened with a different pet who was not trained as they were left next to a road. If anyone GENUINELY cared about my pet they would not have left my dog near a road or they would have left a bowl of water. I suspect this did not happen because a) after someone bothered to go to our campsite they saw my dogs were left with water, food, a bed and shade or b) nothing happened because no one cared about the welfare of my dog and rather just wanted to dob us in for a fine. I found this to be an extremely strange experience from a caravan park. We’re quite seasoned traveler’s and have stayed in many caravan parks and generally find the staff/owners to be very helpful and family oriented so this was an extremely strange and awful experience. I think the staff must’ve realised they made a mistake as they granted us a refund quite quickly after claiming they couldn’t provide our refund for the final night. We have chosen to leave early as we are disappointed in this park and personally quite hurt they think we’ve left our pets without water. If you’re considering staying here, I would suggest looking for an alternate park with better values and care for all their customers.
Perfect spot, accommodating and helpful when we were in a tight spot, the tiny house cabin was great.

We have stayed here in our caravan the last 3 times we have been to Adelaide  feels like your in the country side but can get to city in about 20min. Pet friendly,  if mosquitoes like you bring repellent. Happy a pool, and they are building new accommodation which look great on the out side. We plan on coming make in may to stay in a cabin. It's a popular place.
It was a perfect location close to everything. Set in a country setting beautiful views. Very clean and spacious cabin at a great price for our family.

Bon camping, le moins cher pour dormir vers Adélaïde, le bus est à 15min à pieds et emmène dans le centre direct
Douches et toilettes super propres
Beautiful location service is very friendly. Only 20 minutes from the CBD.
A nice and clean park so close to the city of Adelaide. Very convenient and I will certainly be back again.
Don't like car so far away from room and out of sight. VERY thin walls heard bloke next door snoring all night aand crashing round in morning
Excellent caravan park close to the city. Staff very helpful and informative. Our little Moodle Hamish was also a happy little walker!

The brown hill caravan park is in a beautiful setting the staff are very helpful.  The cabins are in a great spot.  The only issue we had was someone needs to get in an clean the cabins the cabins are very dirty they also need Maintenace on the inside.  This park is in a fantastic spot it is a shame it is being let down by untidiness.
Bad internet but fine for sleeping, will stay again!

Fantastic! The caravan park is situated perfectly, only 15min drive from the city but you feel like you are really in the bush. Koalas and other wildlife are easy to find if you go looking.
All park facilities were very well kept, astro-turf sites are a luxury. The park staff are lovely and are happy to help where they can. The park and surrounding area is very dog-friendly, Dog park is less than a 10min walk away.
Although our stay was pleasant, construction works are creating a lot of dust. The Wi-Fi is pretty hit and miss if you are further towards the Southern end of the unpowered sites
I could recommend this Park

We stayed on a powered site for 3 nights sites were very well kept.
Synthetic lawn on annex side was fantastic. Kids loved the pool.
Staff were very helpfull and friendly
Very well done

Schöner Park, leider konnten wir wenig erkunden waren nur 3 Tage dort .Wobei ein Tag ein langer Ausflug auf die Insel war

Würden wieder kommen lg

Fabulous location, quiet and peaceful with beautiful surroundings.   We stayed in a tent on an ensuite site.  While the ensuite could use a bit of a spruce up, we thoroughly loved our stay.  We had no issues with staff, and while the internet was a little scratchy, it was no issue; that's what a holiday is all about.. Highly recommend for anyone who wants to get away and yet be close to the city.

We had an very dissapointing time here and stayed in many different camp places. There is only one kitchen for the whole camp side and its directly at the entrance. So if you are really unlucky, you have to walk 300m to the kitchen and back just to get hot water for the tea. Shower and toilets were very dirty and they clean it only once per day. So not really a pleasent stay…

The surrounds are beautiful with an abundance of birds and beautiful trees but regardless of the natural beauty the experience is marred by some pretty unsettling factors.
There appear to be some permanents who have taken up residency near the toilet block and given their unsavory setup crowding the entry to the toilets along with the obvious signs of drug use we never felt this was an overly safe environment.
Very nice caravan park on the edge of the Adelaide hills but still in the suburbs - unit I got was very nice and clean
Great place to stay. Nice & quiet at night

So let me start with the location ..serene and just so beautiful ... trees birds and the creek .. all magnificent ... a must visit if you have your own caravan or even camper and or tent ... the cabin .. hmmm ... so sad to say toilet was good the shower was good ..no black mold ... but we had no body wash and the floors in the cabin were dirty .. i am not gona make this review a pity party but were not down for holiday or family .. having said that .. i did ring on the monday ... please note our stay started on the sunday we left the tuesday ... i rang to let them know there was no body wash and the receptionist promised she would put some by our door ..got back monday bout 5.30 from what we had to do and there was nothing there ..mind you the temp for that day in adelaide was 29 ... so yep nothing i could do ..we left the tuesday and when we got home as in millicent .. i rang to find out what happened .. she did apologise very much and said she simply forgot ...hmmm tell me small thing not important too fussy i do not know ..but the standards of these places are below parr ..there is no excuse ... i just want a place in adelaide that is pet friendly has onsite cabins ..NO BLACK MOLD ... and good service ....nearly forgot ... on top of this all ..  we had NO phone service plus we could not receive any wifi in the cabin

Such a pretty off the mobile grid place ruined by horrible staff. If you love to be off the mobile grid, no internet, no phone reception, unreliable wifi, with empty wallet syndrome, then this place is for you. Its such a shame its so poorly managed. It has the chance to be worth the fortune they charge you. Powered sites around $90 a night, when they say so,  if you love that idea. The pool will freeze your girls eggs and the boys taddies for future use. Pizza to warm your cockles on a fri/sat night is offered for $$ on site. Bushland is beautiful. Bitter sweet.

Do not stay here. Price gouging for events like VAILO, you will pay whatever she plucks out of thin air. A normal powered site is $51.50/night any time, but during events around $90/night. Wifi is non-existent along with phone reception, and your own internet connection, complete black hole. The only thing it has going is the beautiful surrounding bush. The staff are unhelpful, will pull out any excuse to not refund the deposit paid for future booking (more than a week notice). Stay somewhere else and save your money. The bathroom close to us was very mouldy and outdated and full of asbestos (according to the sign). No long-term staying here we were told on arrival, and yet our neighbour had been there since Feb 24 and another had been 10 years. The bunkhouse is nice to stay in (we stayed in it Jan 24), and cheaper than a powered site over "events". Staff appear cranky during the day and unknowledgeable after hours. They are dishonest and untrustworthy. Stay 10nights elsewhere for the same price as 6 nights at BHCCP.
Very good  4 the money  neat an tidy  good  size lots
Great

The caravan park is undergoing upgrades and improvements since new management and the place is looking fresh and appealing so why have i scored low stars is because i have not experienced rooms, i have only visited and i have not stayed at the resort. Upon booking enquiry a few months ago a staff member was what you may call passive aggressive behaviour which sadly has doubt me moving forward so i won't stay there. The location is in a valley and there is none to 1 bar mobile signal telstra and optus that i have frequently experienced, the caravan park have starlink to provide customers internet access but it appears to not be the best from what i hear. If you have your own starlink you will be fine and your mobile theu wifi calling on modern phones will provide you with mobile coverage. It is close to mitcham shopping centre about 7mins drive. It is sheltered but during winter and spring with shade from the sun it can be cold, even when having a coffee at the kiosk which is a nice cafe on the caravan park. Down the back of the caravan site there is an unsightly view at toilet block of vehicles tarpaulins vans congregation horrible look and would scare off anyone who would not want to be put near them plus a further walk to the other amenities for toilet and shower use which are better. The pool doesnt look inviting as to its location and size, i think that the pool and play area could be dramatically improved and with all the cabin upgrades, new cabins under construction and new site flooring i hope they have funded to be spent on that too. A noisy buggy petrol or diesel spoils the natural beauty location and although they have another golf style battery buggy for cleaners etc i wouldn't be happy to hear that noisy thing first thing in the morning or to wakup to. Belair national park caravan park has so much more to offer,  full mobile 5G 4G signals, tv reception,  walk into national park free and 10mins walk flat to picturesque views lake and only minutes drive to shops coles etc.
Cabin was small and floor had holes an rips everywhere an there's nothing for teenagers it needs a games room an maybe a big pillow atleast something for the kids

First visit to Brownhill Creek Tourist Park, great site, very friendly & helpful staff.
Great coffee shop, heaps of space.
Very clean facilities.
Love it, highly recommend.
Close to everything, will come back next visit.

Beautiful holiday park in Mitcham, close to city and shopping centre.
Friendly staff and great coffe at 1954 cafe
Friendly staff, great location. Mobile coverage isn't the best but you can use wifi calling when connected to the parks free wifi.

Great experience with how clean and tidy it was but unfortunately when leaving we left 6 out of 10 of our jack daniles behind in the fridge along with left over dinner, called a few hours after leaving and was told nothing was found in the fridge even though we locked to door before vacating. (The owner told me she cleaned it and found nothing) ||Bit confused on where the drinks had gone considering the doors were locked until cleaner arrived.

We stayed on the grass powered site in our caravan. Such a pretty park. We'd grabbed a last minute booking and the lady that served us was very helpful.  The park is quiet and the creek running by is an added bonus! Great spot to stay for hiking.

This is a really good park in a quiet peaceful scenic environment very clean amenities great park to manoeuvre easy access to sites has a good cafe and staff are friendly Ally at the reception desk was so helpful with giving so much information on area and things to see and how to move around this is a excellent place
Great van Park in a handy location. Really helpful staff.
Really nice caravan park on the outskirts of Adalaide. Definitely one of the prettiest parks I've stayed at. A1 amenities.
No Wi-Fi no phone reception most of the time.
Great staff
This place is located in few minutes drive from the city. Unbelievable

We love the low key, rural feel to this park. It is so close to the city and the new grass matting is a great inclusion on the  majority of the powered sites. The on-site Cafe and  nearby walking tracks are fantastic. Very dog friendly too.
Really really nice

Only positive's would be it’s quiet and the bird life, upon arrival setup of power and water was difficult to locate (full extension lead to power supply approx 20 metres) and water supply was inadequate and had to rely on RV supply, the amenities were a disgrace even after the cleaners came were still the same! For $48 a night it’s is extremely poor and forget Wifi and mobile services totally inadequate. Also as a footnote the new toilet block at the very end of the park had campers in a tent next to the toilet block entry with a aggressive dog on a lead but was long enough to make contact, have tried to contact the park about this but no response.
Very quiet location in a bush type setting. Would not know you are next to city. Toilets tidy with passkey entry.

Have stayed here twice and first time staying was in a budget cabin which was quite spacious and had two set a of bunk beds and a queen bed. Enjoyed the view as it was overlooking the creek which was peaceful. The second time staying was in one of the tiny houses which was a definite favourite compared to the budget cabin. Highly recommended trying to book one of these if you are planning on staying here. Has a full kitchen, shower and toilet. Dining table and the view of the hills is beautiful. Campfire site just behind it with bbq facilities for larger groups as well as a playground along side. Checking in is simple and a complete run through of all amenities and activities are explained. Over all a tidy, comfortable and friendly environment to stay.
LOVE this caravan park. It's beautiful and nestled away between mountains. The walks are breath taking and the facilities are clean and in order. AMAZING 👏
We stayed at an ensuite powered site. The site and facilities are pretty good. The only downside is that the mobile signal is very weak. The park provided wifi through starlink is also slow.

Stayed on a powered site okay size ground is gravel. We where next to a amenities block which should be closed down it leaks have never seen something so old and bad it had 2 showers and 2 toilets.There is a bigger amenities block bit of a walk but much better.|It is close to a shopping centre and there are walking tracks outside the park Adelaide is only 20 mins drive.|Staff at front desk friendly swimming pool quite small okay kids playground.

The park has no phone signal.  I've stayed there a number of times for work, and their "free" Wi-Fi is practically non existent.  When I raised it with them, they were un concerned.  If you have your own WiFi with you it's probably a great place to stay, but relying on theirs is a nightmare.

The caravan park is so pretty, the only let down was there was no toilet or shower for the disabled, as I'm.in a wheelchair, ( Hubby probably should have checked with them 1st),
was a little disheartening.
But overall a very pretty caravan park.
Great city fringe caravan park. Amenities are good and clean. It's set in a valley and surrounded by bushlands. Great for hiking and biking. Local pubs and shops close by

Just had a stay for gather round on an unpowered site. We had such a good time, the amenities were cleaned twice a day and always clean. Staff were lovely didn’t bother us our whole stay will definitely be back next year for hopefully another gather round weekend cheers guys

Was given a sloping unpowered site - very uncomfortable.
The park appeared overbooked.
It was giving trailer park.

Second visit but bad experience not likely to return, booked three nights and left after second night, noisy, rowdy feral neighbours and the park keepers do nothing to manage those behaviours, no credit offered as they say they cant control their patrons, the sites are dusty from the poor road and next to no internet cover.  What used to be a tv room is now a unit with no signage, we as well as others had walked in to this room not knowing it had been converted,   we heard kids telling their parents they walked in on the same couple while they were having s...., sheer laziness all round for their shocking service.

Lovely location. Shady nice size sites. We stayed in an ensuite site. Which was clean enough. The pool was well used by the family even though it was icy cool.
It was my observation that the ensuites. And the pool area was looking a bit tired. It was great being able to bring our little dog and while being a responsible dog owner. It would have been nice to find a poop bag dispenser.
Two things I did not enjoy. Was seeing a rat disappear under the ensuite. And the friendliness of the European wasps. Around the sites and pool area.
Other wise a lovely spot to stay with the blend of city and country.
We stayed last year at this campground and kitchen seems to not be clean for whole the time. Mess everywhere, full bins and infested with ants. We were disappointed by this GDay park.

We enjoyed our stay.

The amenities are small but appear well maintained and clean.

The sites had good shade and the valley stays cooler in the week of 40 temps we had.

Was much busier over the long weekend, but not intolerable.

Close to the city and beaches , everything is really within 20 minutes.

The pool is very small, however there is enough room if you are willing to ignore the stare of the backpackers who think they own the pool.

There is little to no phone service and the park Wifi, well you can connect, but I am not sure if it's actually connected to the internet........dial up at best hahahah

Its fabulous to see the wildlife, kookaburra so friendly, but there is starting to be a bit of a rat problem coming up from the creek

Get away from the hustle and bustle of the burbs and surround yourself with the quiet tranquil scenery that is the Aussie outback. Yet the mod cons are only a stone throw away. Staff are fantastic and a big shout out to the park manager Tracy Cains for helping us get sorted and settled in. She made us her first priority. A chill dip in the pool, a relaxed walk through the hiking trails and back to base with a barby on great facilities. Will be coming back again real soon! Thanks BCCP!!

I only planned to stay for one week, but it’s so beautiful here I kept extending my stay and it’s now almost 2 month. The location is beautiful surrounded by bushland. You can see and hear nature 24/7. Koalas, cockatoos, the regular dear family, kookaburras, magpies, kangaroos… I can
recommend the steep walk up to the top of Brown Hill: the sunset is magical and the view over Adelaide is worth it. Thank you Tracy for making guests feel welcome. I’m coming back soon. Mike

I wish I could give better rating but we had food robbed out of the camp kitchen and the staff just said “it is out of our control”. For being close to the city should be more vigilant, we didn’t feel very safe. Our “neighbour” left loads of rubbish which we reported and were told that would be collected the next day. Lovely location but needs more looking after, it didn’t feel very homely, also the ground was very hard to peg a tent, sites should be sorted better, by caravan and tents suitability. Many permanent residents as well.
A taste of the bush 15 mins from the city. Great staff,  quiet patrons, pool to cool off in and bush walking options. Really happy I stayed there & will be staying again.
Fabulous Cafe 1954!

Standard cabin in a lovely location. All the facilities you'd want and more. Cafe and kiosk. Pizza some nights. Uber also delivers there. A little dearer than most of my accommodation but you're probably paying for the location. Staff are friendly and it's clean.
Mobile sognal struggles in the shadow of Brownhill and I couldn't connect to the park wifi. That's ok. I wasn't there for the wifi.
Great place to stay! Just out of the city but it feels like you are miles away.
Cabins were very sub standard mould and spiderwebs visible in every part of the cabin
Lovely spot, don't even know your in the city. Lovely staff there also. Recommend this park 👍🏼

We stayed in a powered site from 17 - 28 December 2023. The location was amazing. As soon as we turned onto the local road I went into holiday mode. We absolutely loved the bush setting, the birds were a godsend, the trees and grass and shade were a beautiful site for sore, tired burnt out souls needing a rest. Considering that the park was quite full, it was very quiet.
Tracey explained how to use the amenities , near the laundry, which were clean and spacious. We were close to the Zoo, Mitchum shops and family were only 25 min drive away. We didn’t get to sit around camp much as we were out and about exploring the area.
We had one minor issue with our site which was rectified by Tracey who was very gracious and accomodating to our issue.
The wifi is practically non existent, this really needs attention.

Cafe 1954, while it was great to have coffee onsite the quality of food and beverage was a bit hit and miss. One day we got hot coffee the next it was lukewarm. I had a cooked breakfast which was warm when I got it, the egg whites were still raw in some places, the mushrooms were dry and the bacon was blah. My hubbys pancakes were left to sit on the bench near the window while my breaky was being finished. We had breaky burgers another day which were nice. I would suggest that should be a bit older or employ a senior and can move faster in the kitchen as the young girl in there was very slow. All staff very friendly though.

We would gladly stay here again.

While the location and holiday atmosphere of Brownhill Creek Caravan Park are undeniably impressive, nestled beautifully in the midst of nature and the jungle, our accommodation experience was unfortunately not up to par. The most glaring issue was the cleanliness of the property. Despite the stunning natural surroundings, the state of our accommodation was disappointingly substandard. The property was filthy. This lack of attention to cleanliness extended to the towels provided, which were dirty and unsuitable for use. It’s a real shame because the park’s location is truly exceptional, offering a serene and beautiful escape into nature. The potential for a perfect holiday experience was there, but the poor state of the accommodation significantly detracted from what could have been an idyllic stay. It’s my hope that the management will address these issues, as this place has all the makings of a wonderful retreat, if only more care was given to the upkeep and cleanliness of the accommodations
Best place
Very peaceful and comfortable
Great location, and most needs catered for in units. Was quiet. Koalas wandering about was highlight. Would stay here again.
Beautiful and peaceful

This is such a great place to stay, very central to everything. Lovely bush atmosphere in the middle of suburbia. My only complaint was the cabins were not exactly clean, bathroom floor needed mopping, cracked tiles on the floor, cobwebs all around the room (which I cleaned myself) and windows and flyscreens needed a good clean. I understand they are short staffed but cleaning should be a priority. Otherwise,  I would stay there again.
Nice quiet park, I have an ensuit site so can't complain, phone reception isn't good but works OK on the WiFi that the park supplies
Well setup and local to Adelaide Caravan Park.
Very convenient location. Nearby Ed hotel is great. Transport options to city are good.
Amazing place to stay would recommend
Great caravan park. We use unpowered. Had issue with wifi they are trying to fix

We had a powered site for 5 nights. Such a great spot. Well maintained clean park located short drive into the city. Nearby is mitcham Park and the small shopping centre ( especially the foodland) was great for groceries. Do yourself a favour and head to Highgate Hills samrat Indian cuisine for takeout or dining experience it was the best we have ever had. Caravan park is like in a rainforest feel of an are with beautiful easy walking trails nearby. Park managers and staff are all very friendly. Absolutely 5 star experience

No wifi at all and they keep on saying it was my phone which it wasn’t- other guests told me they had the same problem - staff are rude and not helpful at all especially the husband of the woman that’s works at the from very patronising and grumpy

It’s a safety issue having no internet my family were frantic not knowing where I was.

Beautiful setting though
Great service at the reception desk and beautiful location but the cabin needed alot of upgrading.
Had an awesome time away. Great location with heaps of walking trails, hotel down the road etc. Very quiet and relaxing.  Highly recommended.
Budget cabins well past their best by date.

Enjoyed a hike on the trails today, Kate afternoon. Idealism setting with gorgeous flora and fauna. Birds active at dusk, spotted a Koala in a Eucalyptus tree and enjoyed the wildflowers. Shared stories with others on the trails also appreciative of nature   TeresaB Online from Canada , visinting this Oasis.  Did I say GORGEOUS trees! ❤️💕. We’ve stayed here 10 years ago and brought other visitors from the USA here too

Highly recommend the experience. Ron and Teresa Brown from Adelaide and British Columbia 👌😁
A great caravan park with great facilities, would definitely recommend this place to everybody.

The location was nice and quiet. I found Koala’s nearby.
Unfortunately I did not like having a light shine in my eyes. (Tenting in an unpowered sight and the light was bright in my campsite)
Nobody explained that there was a touch pad to get into the toilet and shower room that you have to touch before entering the code. I was looking for a non electronic system(these where around in other parts of the campground). There was a blank screen that I thought you might swipe a card over. Once this was explained to me by a friendly camper it was easy to use.

My other big complaint was the wifi it hardly worked even when I was down by the office. This was actually a big deal. For $40 in an unpowered tent sight I expected this to work.

The bathrooms were good, the cooking areas were clean and the playground was nice if you have kids. No idea what the cabins where like.

The wifi was and the light was the major drawbacks. Showers were good, with plenty of room, would have been nice to have more room to put things down.
It was nice to have grass to put my tent on as well.

The caravan park was ideal in many ways, running stream, lush bushland and the wild life were breath taking. It isn't til later you realise there was NO phone coverage, very very limited Wifi. The toilet block near me consisted on 1 shower and 2 toilets. Which were ALL unclean, plaster coming off walls. The one sink seemed blocked I went to report this to the office and was told it was too expensive to get a plumber on the weekend. Very disappointed.
We were visiting my daughter, so we saw it from a different perspective . Both rate a 5 star review.

Stayed in a cabin. Whatever you do don’t look under the bed!! It’s disgusting. Also absolutely zero wifi around most of the park. They say it has google mesh through out but I did multiple speed tests and many of the campsites have zero wifi. I also spoke to others staying there and they said the internet is really bad, very spotty at best. Tracy at the front desk was actually cruel when I brought this up as an issue as I wanted to stay in a tent site for 9 days. Apparently you can not stay for longer than 4 nights so don’t come here for a 2 week or even 1 week holiday. They also refuse refund even if for a valid reason like no wifi on site. After video footage proving there was no internet they charged me $100 cancellation fee for staying zero nights. Really unpleasant experience and the staff are cold and nasty. It was apparent this issue gets brought up quite a lot but their zero refund policy is said to all. Just don’t book here, it’s not worth nasty staff and bad amenities.

Terrible internet / phone reception.
Offers wifi. Doesn't reach to the "slums" (Unpowered sites).
A couple of wifi repeaters would be awesome.
It's a great little spot right on the edge of Adelaide. Wake up to the sound of the birds. Great toilet and shower facilities.

Cabins are clean on the surface, but lots of cobwebs, under bed was next level. Very old. Need some refurbishment badly. Dirty mattresses.
Beautiful bush surrounds. Caravanning yes, cabins not so much.
There was blood on the sheets and it was quite gross, it stunk like fly spray and nothing looked cleaned properly. Not to mention the service being very poor!
Look out for the bandicoots
Beautiful place. Nice and rel a xing
The sheets were dirty and in made

Toilets full of spiders, leaking, not clean, no phone reception at all not even an SOS with Telstra, (OHS issue) no internet, no TV reception. Price during the week was $28 & on weekend put it up to $45. Robbery! On the up side close to city, large sites, pool, cafe. That’s the 2 stars. We won’t be back. Expect much more from a Gday park.
Update - We did go back to try it again in August 2023. Stayed at pool end in powered site. Amenities close to office have been updated, wifi and telephone signal need improving a lot we had better signal on the Nullabor Plains!, TV reception better. Staff were lovely, park very clean. To the park let us know when you invest in a signal booster like other parks and we may return.

Great caravan park - downside is lack
of mobile phone connection, WiFi service available but not real strong.

We recently stayed here for several weeks. The ensuite sites were a decent size and the ensuite itself was very generous. There was heavy mould that we sprayed  and tried to clean up for our comfort. The staff cleaned the ensuite fortnightly and changed the shower curtain although the fan/walls/mould/spider webs were not addressed during our stay.  There were several well-equipped camp kitchens both indoor and outdoor with fridges, pizza ovens, bbqs and television near the playground. Firepit and cafe open regularly and open on friday and saturday nights for 1 hour for dinner - albeit only basics. We found the staff to be helpful and friendly. As others have mentioned, the downside was the internet and phone service. We have a satellite that we travel with and could not get any reception from either their wi-fi or ours.
Overall, we enjoyed the park but would definitely not stay longer than a week next time and would make sure that we had no work requiring internet during our stay.

Wish we read the reviews before booking... ensuite sites should be demolished. Floor to ceiling full of mould that they are aware of as they have just painted over it. Exhaust fans don't work as they are full of inch thick dust. Told reception who couldn't care less "ill get someone onto it" I replied you need to rip them apart. Warrants a call to the health department. No offer of a partial refund to only pay a standard site, which should have been offered. Coffee van opens at 9, lady was still setting up at 920... just standing there on her phone as people qued up... a real shame as location is great and overall a beautiful park. Just DO NOT book an ensuite site... you can't say you didn't know as it was clearly all painted over.

Stayed at the park in May with family.  Our ensuite was disgustingly filthy and rundown. Park in general was disappointing. No wifi, internet service non existant, too bad if you had an emergency and needed to call in or out. Staff unaccomodating when we had an issue after our stay. They were rude and disrespectful with other people's property.  Appalling customer service from Tracey and Denise apparently two managers..they offered no help to rectify the issue. They're happy to take your money and any items left behind..wouldn't recommend them or the park  unfortunately.  A very disappointing holiday to say the least ..
Great place ! So close to the city. This is a hidden GEM !
Lovely place to visit or stay
definitely coming back

The Manager called Julie is quite an unpleasant woman. Not friendly, comes off as being aggressive.
The only reason I did a 2 star review was the pool is very nice. Nice seats to sit on and very pretty surroundings.
The toilets are not cleaned. Spiders and spiders webs are everywhere. The mop is in a bucket just for show. It never gets wet.
I tripped on their speed humps while out walking and then  an elderly lady fell over and injured herself badly. The Manager Julie just pretended it didn't happen and sat in her office. The elderly lady broke her nose and jaw. Wasn't even acknowledged by Julie. We had a problem with our towing vehicle (rear ended) and it was just "get your caravan out of the site by 10am!" "We're full "
The young woman who runs the cafe is snarly as well
I don't recommend this caravan park, only because the staff are not nice.
BTW, there is no telephone signal, no internet. You can't get any calls and there's no way to access the internet. Their wifi is 1 meg but when you log on when you wake up, it says you've already used half your allowance and apparently you've been accessing it for 3 hours previously while you've been asleep? It's so slow it's a waste of time. Being in Adelaide we expected to be able to catch up on business and family but no signal at all. Very annoying
